meta_title: Car Rental in Los Angeles, CA — Find & Compare Options
meta_description: Looking for car rental in Los Angeles? Explore pickup locations at LAX and across the city. Find the right car for your LA trip.
h1: Car Rental in Los Angeles, California
intro_paragraph: Los Angeles is one of the most car-dependent cities in the world, and renting a car is the most practical way to experience everything the region has to offer. Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the highest-volume rental locations in the United States, with all major suppliers represented and a wide selection of vehicle types available. Beyond the airport, rental locations are spread across the city including Burbank, Long Beach, and central neighborhoods. A rental car opens up the full breadth of Southern California — from the beaches of Santa Monica and Malibu to the deserts of Joshua Tree, the mountains of Big Bear, and the wine country of Santa Barbara, all of which are straightforward road trips from the city.

faq.question: Where is the car rental center at LAX?
faq.answer: All on-airport rental car companies at Los Angeles International Airport operate from the LAX Consolidated Rent-A-Car facility (LAX-C), a dedicated off-site rental center. A free shuttle bus connects the facility to all LAX terminals around the clock. The journey from terminal to rental center typically takes 5 to 15 minutes depending on traffic. Follow signs in the arrivals area for the Rental Car Shuttle.

faq.question: Do I need a car in Los Angeles?
faq.answer: Los Angeles is largely built around the car, and having a rental vehicle significantly expands what you can see and do. Public transport exists but is limited in coverage compared to the spread of the city. A car is effectively essential if you plan to visit beach communities, the Hollywood Hills, Griffith Observatory, or take any day trips out of the city to destinations like Joshua Tree, San Diego, or Santa Barbara.

faq.question: What is the minimum age to rent a car in Los Angeles?
faq.answer: Most major rental suppliers in Los Angeles require renters to be at least 21 years old. Drivers aged 21 to 24 are typically subject to a young driver surcharge. Some suppliers do rent to drivers aged 18 to 20, though fewer vehicle categories are available and surcharges apply. Minimum age policies vary between suppliers, so confirm at the time of booking.
